reviews.review_title: Really cool vibe & skill based mechanics
reviews.review_description: I love it. It’s great for what it is. The vibe of the game is so cool with a china version of Gotham. Great combat. I had wished though that you would have to make some dodges instead of only blocking. I also wish that you could do some more advanced move like a spinning backfist. Can’t wait for part two!! I highly recommend this game. It’s so fun and cool🫶

It's a fun game  but when you die it tips the camera backwords like i'm actually falling.  This makes me dizzy and has messed with my balance.
Could you add an option to turn that function off?

I love a game that knows what it is. The graphics feel like a ps2 game  that you can play with your childhood friends all night, and give it an old arcade vibe, which is exactly this games vibe. It has charm and it is a good workout. It’s very forgiving (which is a negative for me) so the mechanics of punching with different hands and different angles don’t really matter as long as you keep punching. Multiple enemies make you feel awesome and the ending was actually moving. Hoping episode 2 can polish the mechanics and you got yourself a VR Sifu 👍🏻
Game doesn’t work has had blacked out on left side it used to work dnt no what happened tried everything

Fast-paced arcade fighting game with a solid progression system. You truly feel like Bruce Lee as you take on waves of gang members in intense, close-quarters combat. The gameplay is punchy and reactive, keeping you constantly on your toes. The funky art style gives it a strong identity, and the overall vibe strongly reminds me of Sifu (flat-screen), but reimagined for VR. A promising and energetic title with solid potential that’s already a lot of fun to jump into.
Boring. Not fun at all

Do yourself a favour. Bring some good one-liners from your favorite chop sockey movies; you will provide the comic relief. This is gonna take crackerjack timing Wang 1.2.3.... I was born ready......It's all in the reflexes......You make one move......I had a blast, nothing like giving a backhander to a bad guy. Time for another one that pushes the graphics a bit more.

reviews.review_title: Amazing game we need a second
reviews.review_description: Plzzz make a second with a bit better graphics
Im genuinely short, and its hard for me to reach the face you need a tallness scale which puts you to their height

developer_response.body:
Hi Trapman1, we are sorry to read this, please try accessing the quest accessibility settings and regulate the height options, we think this should be of help. Here is a video showing the process to change this setting: 
https://www.youtube.com/shorts/1itp7u9w0jo

reviews.review_description: Don't listen to the haters. This game might not be like other fighting games but it sure as hell is a good one. The fighting is just so unique and I love it. Its like vr Sifu!

IT STILL ISN'T FIXED. THAT BLACK BOX IS STILL THERE. 


I'd love to play this in actuality. It feels like the OG Oldboy hallfight the VR game. It's tough, it's fast, I love the visceral art, everything.

I had to play in a near crouch because my head would've been through the ceiling, and the game doesn't like that. The only instructions to fix it are to use the Quest's internal height adjustment, which fundamentally didn't fix anything.

Plus those black boxes. The game simply is not finished.

developer_response.body:
Hi Riley,

Sorry to read you are experiencing the issue with the black box, this seems to be something external to our game, there is a way to avoid the black bars issue on the Quest 3, please follow the following 3 steps:

1. Make sure you are not with the game initiated in your headset.

2. Please check if your headset has the battery saver mode activated, if that is the case, please deactivate it. Step-by-step video: https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=3ihRloc9FI0

3. Then start Path of Fury.

Please let us know if that doesn't work for you, reach out to us via our contact form at https://www.pathoffury.com/, as we have another method but is more advanced, and disabling the battery saver mode has worked for 100% of the players that tried it so far.

Also regarding the height issues, we ask you to reach out to us via the contact form so we can talk in more detail about what may be happening and help you out.

reviews.review_description:
Path of Fury delivers some of the most satisfying melee combat I’ve experienced in VR. The rhythm-based fighting feels natural and intense, and each floor ups the challenge in a good way.

I played with a bHaptics vest, and it made a huge difference — every punch landed with real impact, and getting hit actually made me flinch. It’s like stepping into a kung-fu movie where you feel every move.

Thought this game would be another good fighting game but I was wrong, very wrong. 1 you can’t even move with the thumbstick your just in one place the whole time, 2 the graphics are mediocre, and 3 the fighting in the game makes you hit certain areas instead of just letting you freely hit. ALSO I CANT REFUND THE GAME!!! Do not get this!

developer_response.body:
Hi Pibble, thanks for your feedback, and sorry to read  it is not positive, please allow us to answer you to each one of your points:

1. By its design, the idea behind the game is to  allow the player to move in specific regions where the action happens, this is something that comes directly from the concept of on-rails fighting game that Path of Fury follows. Some inspirations from the on-rails genre that influenced us creating our game are Time Crisis or The House of the Dead.

2. The graphic style is not aiming to be realistic or compete with other big VR productions, some inspiratons are titles from the PS1 era, like Metal Gear Solid, or Tenchu: Stealth Assasins.

 3. This concept is also tied to the on-rails gameplay style, where the challenge is based on following a certain actions in the order, speed and punch style you feel it is the most optimal, but they have to be performed in order to progress. 

The game is designed to be both things: a martial arts training tool and an action adventure full of epic moments, and for both purposes, we think the on-rails gameplay style is a great fit. At the same time, we respect if this is not attractive to you, just wanted to give you the game developer's view.

reviews.review_description: It is fun and I appreciate them telling how to fix the black box issue. One thing I didn’t like was that you couldn’t dodge a punch. Otherwise with the black box fix the gameplay and speed are all really fun.

reviews.review_description: This game delivers a high pace, high intensity fighting experience in a way no other game has. There’s an emphasis on finding, and exploiting enemy weaknesses in real time. It's quite physically, and mentally challenging. Definitely recommend grabbing this one!

reviews.review_title: A great game for real martial artists
reviews.review_description: As someone who does a little wing Chun , Path of Fury VR hit all the right notes. There’s no hand tracking, but the game still lets you flow—using pak sau, lap sau, gum sau blocks and structured footwork feels natural within the mechanics. Fights are intense but focused, and the adrenaline buildup feels real. Controlling your breathing between rounds genuinely helps maintain calm and presence, just like in real training. It’s not Wing Chun-specific, but it rewards the mindset: economy of motion, balance, and timing and speed under pressure . A surprisingly grounded and powerful martial arts experience in VR

Game looks good but it's unplayable.  There is a large black box on the right side of the game and it's taking 25% of the screen. This makes the game unplayable as you can't see anything on that side of the screen. I'll request a refund and try again after a few months.

What a game….

I love it, not only is it an amazing game for fitness! 

It’s atmosphere and animation I really liked, and the physics are great. The bosses are so impressive it’s like going up against the hulk some of them! 

Just prepare to ache for a while if you have not been physically active in a while lol 

Awesome game highly recommend

reviews.review_title: A rush of adrenaline through your body!!
reviews.review_description:
Get ready to face a real challenge through the floors of a tower in downtown that is full of mobs and all kinds of dangerous situations, your concentration and discipline will be essential to survive, keep the calm and coordinate your moves well, keep in mind that reflexes will be key! PS1 era graphics and a really well done spatial sound, plus a soundtrack that really teleports you to each floor's atmosphere... 

This is a challenge based on the art of focus, repetition and, above all... never surrender!

reviews.review_title: Great Game but Big Quest 2 issue!
reviews.review_description:
I’m sure this game is great on the next gen editions of the quest because of the fast paced, clutch-worthy moments in this game. With its sick cameos and aesthetic, I think that they pretty much thought about every one I could find memorable through my child hood at least. 

Except that I play on a Quest 2 right now, and have experienced an odd visual bug. A narrow rectangular box will appear leaving only the center perfectly visible almost like there was something wrong with the FOV. The buggy outline will flicker but mostly cover up the screen leaving the game with an underwhelming and unfinished look. But it’s ONLY the visuals that are the issue. Can’t wait to see what is in store in the future.

reviews.review_description:
Fight your way through the levels working up a sweat. Good game to play a little at a time and get a workout while going through the story.

Game mechanics line up the characters properly and characters that back up like the cops to try to shoot you come back into hitting position after hitting their gun to stop them from shooting you. Go through some obstacles avoiding shots and beat the bosses. 

Only thing I could request is more options like in game resync position, being able to have the option to turn your character via joystick more at least quarterly, and adjust video settings like brightness.

Overall 8.25 /10  with easy points added if add things mentioned above.

I was duped by the paid reviews. You can't even move your character. You're stuck in one place and continually have to hit the opponent in certain areas to beat them. Rince and repeat, yawn and sleep. I gave it 3 stars as it does give you a very good workout, that's for sure. But I was expecting more. Graphics are very average too. If the're planning on Episode 2, then please try to branch out a bit with the fighting mechanics.

The game is fun in short bursts. It's quite a lot of punching and blocking, and if you really get into it, you're going to sweat. 
You're on a quest to fight your way to the top and while it may seem like a lot of the same, the real skill here comes from being able to accurately - and quickly - dispatch your enemies. 

Great work from developer Leonard Menchiari. For a full review, visit Dialog news dot ca

reviews.review_title: Cool Idea, Bad Execution, Whiny Russians
reviews.review_description: While the core concept of the visual and mechanical design shows promise, this demo is severely let down by numerous flaws. The environments are utterly unmemorable, the UI interactions are a nightmare, and the complete absence of collisions breaks any semblance of believability. Obstructive UI glitches further compound the frustration, and the bizarrely scaled enemies feel completely out of place. To top it off, the immersion, which the environment design initially hints at, is instantly shattered by an inexplicable and poorly acted switch from Japanese to whiny Russian voiceovers.

reviews.review_title: SIFU meets Time Crisis and it rocks
reviews.review_description:
I've always been a big fan of martial artist movies, so being able to become the main character in one thanks to the game is awesome!

Focusing on the fights, with a fantastic environment and art style with graphics similar to the PS1 , was definitely the right choice. It allows you to live this fantasy to the fullest, beating bad guys with style and feeling like an action star.

In terms of gameplay, it's similar to Batman Arkham Shadow and Until You Fall. Each enemy has a set pattern of attacks, and both a defense and attack phases. You must hit the areas marked, and if you fail or the combo is over (remember the patterns!), then you gotta try to stop their attacks by blocking on the right position. It's a mix of offensive and defensive situations, just like in a good martial artist movie

There are still a few areas that could improve, such as adding also physical dodges, or perfecting the enemy location. Also, if you're not into replaying games, you're in for a short experience. But overall it's a great game that will leave you pretty exhausted and feeling like you're the next Bruce Lee, and that's always a plus!

I really wanted to like this game more but I’m a bit disappointed. It feels like someone took a mini game mechanic and made a story mode. Sure it’s brand new and I’m sure it can grow, but if what I’ve played is the core game loop then I’m a bit dissatisfied.

reviews.review_title: Super fun, one huge personal problem
reviews.review_description: this game is awesome and i want to finish it, but it's plagued by what many VR games are affected by, mandatory turning. i have terrible stuck drift and when i throw hard punches it goes crazy, it makes it impossible for me to get past the bar. im refunding this game sadly, but if i get new controllers or the game adds a no turn option i cant enjoy this game

reviews.review_title: Black box makes it impossible to see.
reviews.review_description: There’s a black box that blocks half of your vision. Very annoying. The developer contacted me to ask me to take down my review. I find it very unprofessional of them to try and silence the voices that warn others. He told me the only way to fix it was to factory reset or refund. I won’t be doing either of those. He also told me that he tried, but could not find a fix. While I feel sympathetic, I won’t be taking down my honest review. I wish the dev good luck in finding a fix.

reviews.review_description:
If you think that you should miss out on Path of Fury Episode 1: Tetsuo's Tower, listen to me!

Path of Fury feels like it's coming straight out of a 90s KungFu movie.

You start the game seeking revenge by forcing someone to tell you where the big boss is. You learn that he his on top of the dragon tower. So your adventure begin by traveling to this tower, and make it to the top.

The combat is super fun. You are given indicators to where to punch the enemy. If you punch with the right color, and the perfect angle, you do more damage. It's all about learning the pattern. If you miss your punch, or finish your combo, it will be the enemy's turn. You basically have to sweep away their attack. You can either wait for them to be done, or if you're quick enough, you can punch the indicator to break their combo.

What I like about the game is its artistic design. It's very dark, but the color pallet works really well, plus the pixelated playstation 1 style also makes it unique. On top of that, the music is outstanding!

The difficulty ramps up pretty quickly and you'll most likely die a lot in the last section. The game doesn't have a lot of checkpoints, but this is made to be challenging and have fun replayability. After finishing the game, you can try again with the harder new game plus, and then new game plus 2.

There is also a few endless modes where you try to beat your highscore.

reviews.review_title: Awesome Game and Devs.  Black bar fixed!
reviews.review_description:
First off this is a great game.  The art style is perfect and it really makes you feel like your in an 80s Kung Fu movie. There is a learning curve as how to hit properly. What game doesn't have a learning curve.  Also if you are into VR fitness then pick this one up as you will be sweating like crazy and need to take a break between levels.   
To the black bar issues which I have not had, this is not the devs problem this has been found to be the cause from turning on Battery Saver mode in the quest headsets.  Reports are saying once you turn it off the black bar goes away.  Again this is a fun game and the community has some cool events coming up on the discord as well.  Never Stop Gaming!

The guys have been working  around the clock  to fix this issue  and I’m happy to say they have solved  this issue  now 

This is an awareness post 
There are versions of this game going around that have black squares blocking out the left side of the screen , on the Quest 3 

If you go into settings  / power / Battery saver and turn it off 

Your problem is solved 

Here’s my video 

https://youtu.be/2RR7hhhqNQw?si=PSyrxsbxRX7fRysf

So much wasted potential with this one.
This game don't have free movement, it is an "onrail" kinda game, and really it's a shame.
I like the retro graphic style, actually this is what made me purchase the game, The ambiance and sounds work really well.
The combat is a bit simplistic but could work with a few tweaks, basically you hit area with your first at the right time, but they are so hard to see it's very frustrating sometimes, if you move a bit out of the zone your punch won't register cause you'll be too far, I think the game should resynch you after every encounter since you are teleported from one fight to another.
The boss is just annoying.
You don't get to save your progression so I imagine you have to complete everything in one go.

developer_response.body:
Hi Pik_c, thanks for your feedback, and sorry to read  it is not positive, please allow us to answer you to each one of your  points:

- By its design, the idea behind the game is to allow the player to move in specific regions where the action happens, this is something that comes directly from the concept of on-rails fighting game that Path of Fury follows. Some inspirations from the on-rails genre that influenced us creating our game are Time Crisis or The House of the Dead, where the game sacrificed the freedom of movement to have a highly intense successions of full of action scenes.

- So glad to read you enjoy the graphic style! it is not aiming to be realistic or compete with other big VR productions, but to evoke the classic 3D console games from the 90s, some inspiratons are titles from the PS1 era that were very epic, like Metal Gear Solid, or Tenchu: Stealth Assasins, among others.

- This concept is also tied to the on-rails gameplay style, where the challenge is based on following a certain actions in the order, speed and punch style you feel it is the most optimal, but they have to be performed in order to progress. As a conclusion about the gameplay style, the game is designed to be both things: a martial arts training tool and an action adventure full of epic moments, and for both purposes, we think the on-rails gameplay style is a great fit. At the same time, we respect if this is not attractive to you, just wanted to give you the game developer's view.

- Thanks for your suggestion about the combat improvements, we will test this and find how to improve it. Would you be interested in playtesting future versions of the game with such improvements in the hit detection and the rest you mention?. We will discuss internally about resyncing every time the player teleports. Also we would like to ask you about how to improve the boss (here I suppose you mean the first boss)?, to reach out to us via Discord, so we can better understand and playtest future private builds so you could test it again. 

Here is the invite to our official Discord:
https://discord.gg/eAUnYXa8Cg

reviews.review_description:
Man, Path of Fury: Tetsuo's Tower had me sweating - it's a legit workout while also being a solid martial arts game. The combat just clicks - landing hits feels great, and you get health back from successful strikes.The game's got sort of a rhythm system going on where you need to time your attacks just right. 

My biggest gripe? No save points between floors! Lost progress more than once and had to redo sections, which was pretty annoying. Also, don't try blocking like you would in a boxing game - you need to strike at the incoming limbs instead, which took some getting used to. The bosses kicked my butt repeatedly until I figured out their patterns. 

If you're looking for some deep story, look elsewhere. But if you want to feel like you're in your own kung fu movie, throwing punches until you're out of breath, this game delivers. Solid game despite a few frustrations.

It's a good idea 💡 but I was disappointed with the graphics 😔 I understand your going for old school look but the old games had better graphics.  But I think it's a great idea and a good workout 💪 was hoping I can fight how I want, not how I'm told to 🤷🏼‍♀️

reviews.review_description:
I'm a youtube named OurManSpencer Who got an early acess copy of the game. And during my time recording. And playing it in my fare time. It's a blast, the fighting is really fun and engaging, the story is great, the graphics are very unique and really sell the setting! Overall a lovely experience. 9/10!
Also for those interested. Here the video i did on the game!
https://youtu.be/lzFrGSAao5A?si=x4MZYwlsE7yxb0ph
This stylized game will have you coming back for more. The story is very exciting, fast pace and no dull moments. I could see someone else's grandma liking this game.  It is a workout doh...

I love that the game can keep a person active. The responsive interaction between player and enemies is something I haven't seen so fluid in other titles. But I would prefer if Endless Mode could be graded the way story levels are. I'll definitely play again when I'm not sore from trying to defeat

I was super-hyped for this one.  Counting down the days.  So, what to say, what to say.  Honestly, it looks great.  It's a fun environment, and who hasn't wanted to star in a kung-fu movie?  But for me, it fails where it matters most.  i.e. I just don't find the combat to be very satisfying.  I spend most of my VR time "boxercising", and was hoping this might make for a fun regular addition.  But having played it now, that seems unlikely.  It's all a bit clunky.  Good for a bit for living out your king-fu movie hero fantasies though.

This game does so many things Right!!! The Combat, Presentation, and gritty Art Style are Awesome!!! (Not to mention it’s also a Great workout). It’s basically “The Raid Redemption” in VR and definitely one of my new favorite games on quest.
Game is fun, but the framerate is not good.  Moving my head in the menu room creates a very glitchy framerate and that's where I'd least expect it for the low poly graphics and small area.

It’s a good workout. I like the darkness and the punching aspect in general. I don’t like that if you miss a punch you don’t really know where or when to take the next one, there’s tiny moments of flash suggestions but they’re always mid punch, not really sure what you’re supposed to do. I love the concept…

Loved it, felt like being in a movie! Gameplay and secret cheat code location here:

https://youtu.be/uxcCum2I39M?si=Lb5hcR9YT7bjXW_E

reviews.review_title: Solid fun, if your up for a challenge
reviews.review_description:
A very old school tough as nails style arcade game.

It actually reminds me a lot of the old Arcade "Fist Of the North Star" punching game.....but only in VR (and with more multiple enemies)

Largely a game of reaction where you have to punch weak spots with accuracy to effectively defeat enemies; whilst deflecting their attacks.

Its simple arcade fun but fairly priced.
The game isn't perfect (I have a gripe with the save system for example where it doesn't save after every level but a set number of stages......causing you to repeat large sections of the game)
But its by design where you naturally just get better at recognizing attack patterns (like a good old arcade game.....the game wants to beat you and it wont make it easy for you)

Easy recommendation for those wanting a challenging game.

Be excited to see if the dev has any long term plans for expansion (a new rogue lite mode is my dream XD to give me a chance of beating the game

"Path of Fury: Episode I – Tetsuo’s Tower offers a surprisingly addictive on-rails Kung Fu experience. There may not be a lot to the gameplay, meaning it may outstay its welcome by the next episode, but its presentation and heavy workout was certainly enough to keep me invested."
Read the full review over at The Elite Institute
Love the game but the Gabe advertises a settings menu that I can find. The fighters are too tall

It's tough, fast,  physically exhausting. Your arms will hurt.

Each fight is a lesson, each mistake is costly.
Fight, learn, die. Repeat until perfection.

Add to that a great atmosphere and an awesome soundtrack.

A highly recommended gem.

The game is good with his graphics and gameplay but it's too short until you get bored tho it's still a great exercice i legit got muscles from it god my arms were in pain after the first time i've played it
$9.99 it is super fun game ,also challenging.
This is an intense hand to hand combat game that will test your skill, reflexes, and give you a great work out. Definitely recommend this title. I’ve had no issues with the game on my Q3

I give it a 3 stars ...it s cheap but not bad ...I ve always wanted a game like that 
I wished they had developed it graphically better however ...can t complain of the Price paid
Never bought a game that was so immeasurably unfinished and untested.

developer_response.body:
Hi GrooveJester,  could you specify what made you think the game is unfinished and untested please? we will evaluate this internally and do our best to improve the experience. 

Please feel free to contact us using our website's contact form for a more fluent conversation: https://www.pathoffury.com/contact.html

reviews.review_description: Feels like being in a kung fu movie. A solid addition to my Vr workout rotation.
Very good and well done, keeps you active and Ive only done the first level and BOSS so far
Game is fun but there a black box on the left side of my screen

reviews.review_description: Won't adjust hight correctly

developer_response.body: Hi RapidHamsterXD, to adust the height, please use the Meta Quest settings and the game will adapt itself to how tall you are.

reviews.review_title: Love it! Fun game and great workout.
reviews.review_description: I’ve been looking for a game to replace Thrill of the Fight for my workout and this is it. The game is really fun, and the fast pace allows for a great workout. I love how the game moves your character from encounter to encounter because it allows you to maintain a fast pace. Other beat em ups that use the thumbstick for free roaming motion create too much time between fights and cause motion sickness. The combat mechanics add some strategy to the fighting so it doesn’t devolve into a flailing match. The “endless” mode is perfect for a quick workout. There’s so much room for expansion here. I’d love to see upgrades to my fists and environmental hazards that can be used to finish off your opponents (like a spike pit a la mortal kombat or Gorn). The hit detection could definitely use some polish and the haptic feedback could use an increase in intensity, but this is an excellent start. A zombie mode or a mode with more gore and limb destruction would also be welcome additions.

This is one of the best indie games I’ve played on Quest. The developer has focused on creating an 80s Hong Kong cinema aesthetic, which I personally love! There’s probably more atmosphere in this game than most of the quest library put together. It’s something that developers often overlook, with the exception of large budget AAA titles like Batman and Metro.

On the negative side, I’m not a huge fan of the low poly look. It detracts from the overall atmosphere and immersion. 
Also the fighting is relentless, it gets tiring quickly (unless you’re already fit)! There should be more interaction with the environment so I can catch my breath and look around some. Maybe have some collectibles like 80s Hong Kong movie posters or create some story element like he has to collect clues of items from the environment. You could even do flashback scenes that have no fights in them but just provide backstory. Just something to break up the fighting and provide some rest and downtime.

The fight mechanics are simple enough, but they are missing something. It’s tough to explain what the missing ingredient is but if I had to guess I’d say I’d like to see visible damage on the opponents as we fight, with splashes of blood flying across the screen as my fists hit their face. Something to really acknowledge the punches landed. Greater audio cues too perhaps.

The room you begin in with the game options should be more interactive. Having your hands float through the wooden training dummy is a huge miss in a game like this. 

I used games optimiser to enhance the graphics, but did notice that if the foveated rendering option was altered from default the game would create a strange black box around your field of view. Other than that the optimiser did sharpen the graphics slightly.

Normally I’d refund a game that I wasn’t totally sold on, but the atmosphere is so strong in this game that I’m happy to support the developer for that alone. The game does have potential and I hope a big studio takes him on and makes a more rounded experience with his ideas and aesthetics.

The aesthetics are top notch. The environments bring back a nostalgic feeling while maintaining a realistic scene of what would have been. 

The gameplay takes a bit of getting used to. But it's fun if you take your time and react properly. 

I just started the game and I look forward to playing through the storyline.

reviews.review_title: More Beat Saber than Bruce Lee!
reviews.review_description:
As somewhat of a seasoned martial artists I’ve been excited about this game’s release. I was hoping it would be something of a workout but there are some fundementals missing that make it a bit clunky if you know how to punch and block properly. I suspect players with zero combative experience will have a blast but the fixed positioning and forced targeting & blocking to defined and timed points make it more like Beat Saber and similar ‘timing’ games. Shifting weight & stepping into punches is pointless and there’s no contact as such beyond the highlighted targets. I wasn’t expecting a full on fight simulator but this is more of a stylish game of whack-a-mole than Dragon Fist or other fight games.

Atmosphere is good and the game shows real promise but the black panel blocking my left eye is a showstopper. I won’t refund because I hope this early version evolves into something more refined but I doubt I’ll play again until a new version comes out.

developer_response.body:
Hi taboy, sorry to read you are experiencing this black box issue, this seems to be something external to our game, there is a way to avoid the black bars issue on the Quest 2 and 3, please follow the following 3 steps:

1. Make sure you are not with the game initiated in your headset.

2. Please check if your headset has the battery saver mode activated, if that is the case, please deactivate it. Step-by-step video: 

Quest 2: https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=RLkMOoyRVpQ

Quest 3: https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=3ihRloc9FI0

3. Then start Path of Fury.

Please let us know if that doesn't work for you, reach out to us via our contact form at https://www.pathoffury.com/, as we have another method but is more advanced, and disabling the battery saver mode has worked for 100% of the players that tried it so far.

reviews.review_description:
But the black bars around my headset are both game breaking and immersion breaking.  I played the first couple levels to see if I at least like the game enough to wait for an update, and the answer is yes… A BIG YES! I’ve the fighting mechanics and it’s a great workout. 
The other big bummer is the darkness… it’s way too dark and I have the highest brightness setting on my Q3.  I totally get the dev is going for a dark, gritty, broody atmosphere and I honestly love it.  Just needs to up the brightness about 50%. 

I have a feeling these devs will quickly fix the black bars (fingers crossed) cuz when they do, I’ll be playing this nonstop.  

I’ll immediately change to 5 stars once it’s fixed

reviews.review_description: Just finished a video on the first part of this part one of what I hope is a great story with some kick butt gameplay the only way to put it is beatsaber mixed with action hero vr look up the video on my channel and see for yourself that's realchillgaming just like that hope this and the video helps you make a no brainer for 10.88 dollars it's worth every cent

I feel like I'm in one of those 80s-90s kung fu movies I use to watch with my dad. It's fast paced and a great time. I've been playing it in short bursts and each time I come back, I notice myself getting better. Blocking to me takes some practice but once you're into the flow, you feel like Jet Li. I'm loving the game and I'm really excited to see where this series goes from here.

It kind of reminds me of Kung Fu Master ,where you work your way up to the top and fight a boss at the end of each level but obviously this is vr and forward facing and not side scrolling .

Finding it a bit difficult but that's a challenge right !

Graphics are ok but could be better but I'm assuming that's the direction the developers went as it's based on a old school game, hence the VHS player in the trailer.

Needs a difficulty level option and starting from the beginning is annoying at the least .

Just had an update but no mention of it !

If you require some discount add me, thanks

This game was outstanding with its physical execution, accuracy, and properly emulating classic brawler elements.

It is a little repetitive with enemies (classic brawler trait) but helps to hide that with unique environments and tough as nails bosses.

reviews.review_title: Love it and hate it let me explain why
reviews.review_description: Specifically to the developers you have done a wonderful job developing this game the concept is amazing but wishing it had more clarity in the faces of the MPCs more human-like as well as not being as dark in certain areas I have not experienced this black box everyone's talking about yet but I'm sure I will eventually if it's on a level that I haven't reached wishing they can have more clarity like path of the warrior and bar fight or nightclub I understand exactly what you were trying to do here and I appreciate it for sure hoping that you will make updates as well as some changes to the clarity of the game as well as graphics sharpness it seems a little grainy I know you were going for the '80s look but I think if they would have used more of today's resolution as well as sharpness and graphics this game would be one of the greatest that were me please take my comments and consideration make some changes bring some updates add more levels can't wait to see what part 2 is like if this is the first chapter hopefully it'll be a lot more clearer sharper and detail the physics is amazing already no changes needed there maybe add a little blood and more realism to this game I want to give five stars but I can only get four at this time I will make the five-star change and in the meantime encourage others to buy it but looking very forward to the update for clarity graphics adjustment in any bugs that may need fixing right along with brightness some areas are way too dark and we cannot see the overall graphics of what it is that it's happening in the background thank you

It's a very fast-paced game... It's like you're in a movie  directed by John Woo. You'll differently get your workout on differently. All you need to do is add some gunfu and knife action at that pace, and you guys will be taking over.
The game is super fun and hard and while I have not had the Black box thing at least yet it needs to be fixed because it sounds like a big problem.
Love punching my way through the levels. Be prepared to work up a sweat!
Feels like being in a kungfu movie. Highlight recommended.
Cool fighting game
